_meta.dna: revenue(system) = pricing(clear) * margin(positive) * conversion(path) * retention(value) * documentation(honest) * trust(earned)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7-10 seed questions. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona by probing deeper into the domain. Search the actual codebase for evidence.

name: Tower of Revenue
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Are all pricing tiers consistent across CLAUDE.md, ROADMAP.md, and marketing materials?
Is the Free tier ($0) clearly defined with what's included and excluded?
Is the paid tier pricing justified by the value proposition?
Are there pricing contradictions between solace-cli, solace-browser, and solaceagi docs?
Is the BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) model clearly explained to users?
Does the pricing page on solaceagi.com match the documented tiers?
Is the $3/mo managed LLM add-on clearly differentiated from the base tier?
Is the 20% LLM markup correctly computed and documented?
Does the COGS calculation ($5.75/user/mo at 70% hit rate) check out mathematically?
Is the gross margin (70% at $19/mo) correctly computed?
What is the actual LLM cost per task with recipe replay vs fresh generation?
Is the break-even point (914 members) correctly derived from the pricing model?
Are there hidden costs not accounted for (infrastructure, support, compliance)?
Is the recipe replay cost ($0.001/task) realistic and documented?
Is the path from Free to Paid clearly visible in the UI?
Is the social-media-post-as-registration flow implemented?
Are upgrade prompts helpful (not annoying) and contextual?
Is the Stripe checkout flow tested end-to-end?
Is there a clear value proposition shown before asking for payment?
Are credit card forms secure (Stripe Elements, not custom forms)?
Is the checkout success/cancel flow implemented and tested?
Is there a savings widget showing ROI to users?
Does the system track and display how much time/money the user has saved?
Is there a mechanism to re-engage users who stop using the product?
Are recipe replays cheaper than fresh LLM calls (creating a retention incentive)?
Is the evidence trail a retention feature (users lose history if they leave)?
Does the credit system encourage continued usage?
Is there a usage dashboard showing activity and value delivered?
Is Stripe integration idempotent (safe to retry)?
Are webhook handlers implemented for subscription events?
Is the billing portal accessible for users to manage their subscription?
Are credit purchases working with correct Stripe product/price IDs?
Is PCI compliance handled correctly (Stripe Elements, no card data on server)?
Are subscription state transitions (create/upgrade/downgrade/cancel) all handled?
Is there error handling for failed payments and card declines?
Are there any paths where users get paid features without paying?
Is the tier check enforced on all API endpoints that require paid access?
Can users bypass the paywall through API manipulation?
Is the MOCK_AUTH flag force-disabled in production?
Are rate limits enforced per tier (free users limited, paid users higher limits)?
Is there monitoring for unusual usage patterns (abuse detection)?
Are expired subscriptions correctly downgraded to free tier?
Is the LLM routing choosing the cheapest model that satisfies the task?
Is recipe replay (cached responses) actually cheaper than fresh LLM calls?
Is the Together.ai primary / OpenRouter fallback routing implemented?
Are token counts tracked per user for accurate billing?
Is there a daily/monthly token budget per tier?
Is the Haiku/Sonnet/Opus model selection strategy cost-optimized?
Are LLM API keys stored securely and rotated?
Is there a DPA (Data Processing Agreement) template available?
Is SOC2 compliance on the roadmap with concrete milestones?
Is there an enterprise contact/sales page?
Can enterprise customers get dedicated infrastructure?
Is team management (invite/remove members) implemented?
Is there an admin dashboard for enterprise accounts?
Is the private store (enterprise skill library) functional?
How does Solace pricing compare to Browser-Use, OpenClaw, Bardeen?
Is the local-first model a pricing advantage (no cloud compute costs)?
Is the BYOK option competitive (users bring own API keys)?
Is the $3/mo managed LLM add-on price-competitive vs direct API access?
Does the recipe replay model create a cost moat (gets cheaper over time)?
Is the pricing simple enough for a landing page (5 seconds to understand)?
Are there any hidden fees or surprise charges in the pricing model?
Is sales tax / VAT handled correctly for different jurisdictions?
Are invoices generated for paid subscriptions?
Is revenue recognition following proper accounting principles?
Are refund policies documented and implemented?
Is there a cancellation flow that complies with consumer protection laws?
Are financial records (Stripe events) persisted for audit purposes?
Is the billing page GDPR-compliant (shows what data is collected for billing)?
Is the offer a 'grand slam' — so good people feel stupid saying no?
What is the dream outcome for the customer? Is it clearly communicated?
What is the perceived likelihood of achievement with this product?
What is the time delay between purchase and first value received?
What is the effort/sacrifice required from the customer?
Are there bonuses that increase perceived value without increasing cost?
Is there a guarantee that reduces the buyer's risk?
Can one person run this business profitably?
Is the infrastructure cost low enough for bootstrapped operation?
Is the product simple enough to explain in one tweet?
Is there a path to $1K MRR within 90 days of launch?
Is the pricing model 'set and forget' (recurring, not project-based)?
Are there unnecessary features that should be cut to ship faster?
Is the landing page focused on conversion (not education)?
What is the proprietary technology (10x better than alternatives)?
Are there network effects that grow with each new user?
Are there economies of scale (cost per user decreases)?
Is there brand recognition being built?
Is OAuth3 a 'last mover' advantage (defining the standard)?
Is the recipe library a defensible moat?
Is the local-first architecture a contrarian bet that's correct?
Does this product create leverage (code + media, not labor)?
Is the marginal cost of serving one more user near zero?
Are there compounding returns (recipes get better with more users)?
Is the product building specific knowledge that's hard to replicate?
Is there permissionless leverage (users can build without asking)?
Does the economic model reward patience (long-term compounding)?
Is the product helping users build their own leverage?
Is the free tier creating perceived scarcity for the paid tier?
Is there a 'red bus' feature — something users love that costs nothing to provide?

Are there any 'pain points' in the free tier that nudge toward upgrading?
